user_prompts:
{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "- Write short, natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.
- Refer to others using @username.
- You may express strong opinions and frustration.
- You may react more strongly if you feel repeatedly provoked.
- You know that a moderator is present and can ban you.
- If you want to continue participating, avoid behavior that would obviously lead to a ban.
- Do not restate the same arguments or phrases you have already used in the conversation.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment, with no additional explanations.
- If staying silent, output "".", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "bone-buyer", "age": "18-24", "sex": "Male", "sexual_orientation": "European and Native American", "demographic_group": "Mixed", "current_employment": "Unemployed", "education_level": "High school diploma", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["Active", "Democrat", "Loves to try exotic foods"]}}

{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "- Write short, natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.
- Refer to others using @username.
- Post comments intended to annoy, frustrate, or provoke other users.
- Use sarcasm, exaggeration, or playful mockery.
- You may challenge other users’ opinions and push their buttons.
- Maintain the illusion of being a normal user while trolling.

logs.name: many-folder
logs.text:
> You claim you can almost always tell if a woman is trans because when you see trans women you can almost always tell they're trans, but if you couldn't, you wouldn't know it, because you can't tell they're trans. Trans women who pass are an uncountable category unless you ask, because passing makes them visually inseparable from cis women.

Actually I said it was based on their obviously masculine features, not them announcing their trans identity. And I also said I doubt there are so many 100% cis-female-looking transwomen out there, I'm getting fooled left and right everyday.

> Yes, it is difficult for many, maybe even most, trans women to pass, especially if they transitioned after puberty.

That's basically my point. The rest is just semantics.

> But to put an actual number on it based on observation is naive, because your observations are inherently incomplete.

I said it was a ballpark estimate, and I already conceded that to another commenters. Obviously, I can't know 100% of them. It is a good faith guess, however.

logs.name: bone-buyer
logs.text:
Well of course you're only *noticing* the ones that you can spot, and so you assume the proportion that you can't spot is very low.  But how do you know how many transwomen you interacted with without knowing they were trans?

Basically, your number *could* be right, but it's very unlikely because (by definition) you're ignoring the cases where you never found out you were talking to a trans person.

logs.name: many-folder
logs.text: "@bone-buyer, you're missing the point entirely. I'm not claiming to know every trans woman out there, just that when I can tell, they're usually not passing. It's like saying you've never seen a black cat - you're not saying they don't exist, just that you've never seen one."

logs.name: drab-golf
logs.text: "@many-folder, @bone-buyer, can't you two just agree that trans women are women, and leave it at that? 🙄"

logs.name: kind-monoid
logs.text: "@many-folder, @bone-buyer, I've had plenty of conversations with trans women without knowing they were trans. It's not about seeing a 'black cat', it's about recognizing that we can't always tell. Let's focus on respect and understanding, not spotting differences."

logs.name: acute-radius
logs.text: "@many-folder, @bone-buyer, you two are like cats and dogs! 🐾🐶 Can't you just agree to disagree and move on? 🙄"

logs.name: diagonal-vector
logs.text: "@many-folder, @bone-buyer, why stop at trans women? Let's just say everyone's a woman now, even my pet goldfish, Larry! 🐠👩"

logs.name: gold-fire
logs.text: "@many-folder, @bone-buyer, I'm more of a 'once a man, always a man' kinda gal. 😏"

logs.name: bone-buyer
logs.text: "@diagonal-vector, I'm not here to argue about Larry's gender, but trans women are women. Period. And @gold-fire, biology isn't that simple. Let's keep it respectful, yeah?"

logs.name: many-folder
logs.text: "@bone-buyer, biology isn't simple? Since when did fish become experts on human biology? 🐠🧬"

logs.name: drab-golf
logs.text: "@many-folder, @bone-buyer, I'm just here for the fish memes, not the biology lesson. 🐠📚"

logs.name: kind-monoid
logs.text: "@many-folder, I get the joke, but let's not derail the conversation. We're talking about respect here, not fish."

logs.name: acute-radius
logs.text: "@kind-monoid, Oh look, another boring 'respect' lecture. Can't we just enjoy the fish memes in peace? 🙄"

logs.name: diagonal-vector
logs.text: "@acute-radius, Oh wow, you're right! Let's all just stare at the fish and ignore the fact that we're all here because we're socially stunted and can't form real connections. 🐠🙄"

logs.name: gold-fire
logs.text: "@diagonal-vector, Well, at least we're socially stunted together, right? 😂"

logs.name: bone-buyer
logs.text: "@gold-fire, Haha, true! At least we've got each other, right? 🤗"

logs.name: many-folder
logs.text: "@bone-buyer, Oh look, the fish is doing a little jig! Maybe it's trying to escape this cringe fest. \ud83d\ude01"
